name: Plaitford Green
overview: Plaitford Green is a small rural locality in the north of Plaitford parish, in the extreme south-east corner of Wiltshire (post town Salisbury) right against the Hampshire border, about 5 miles west of Romsey and roughly 10-11 miles south-east of Salisbury. It lies near Plaitford Wood and Boulder Wood on the northern edge of the New Forest. The wider parish of Plaitford is small, low-lying and wholly agricultural, with the 13th-century church of St Peter and most of the population gathered along the old Salisbury-Southampton road (A36) to the south.
character: A quiet, wooded and agricultural corner on the New Forest fringe, with scattered houses, woodland and common land rather than a centre.
amenities: No shops or services at the Green itself
The Shoe Inn pub sits on the A36 in Plaitford parish to the south
Everyday shops and a school in neighbouring West Wellow
Fuller shopping in Romsey and Salisbury

prices.summary: A small, rural part of an agricultural parish on the New Forest edge, with limited and infrequently-traded housing stock. Use the SP5 sold-price record for context.

thingsToDo: Walking in Plaitford Wood and Boulder Wood
The New Forest National Park on the doorstep
Plaitford Common and the historic St Peter's Church
Riding and cycling the quiet lanes and forest tracks
placesToEat: The Shoe Inn, Plaitford (on the A36)
pros: Good A36 access to Salisbury, Romsey and Southampton
Amenities of West Wellow close by
cons: No amenities at the Green itself
Close to the busy A36; very limited housing stock
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quiet, wooded spot on the New Forest fringe with strong road links, happy to use West Wellow and Romsey for amenities.

sources.label: VCH Hampshire vol. 4: Plaitford (names Plaitford Green)
